rag_rules: Place generated AGREE annex blocks in the target component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Preserve AADL architecture by not modifying existing subcomponents or connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or component type
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by not altering existing subcomponents or connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or component type
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not modify existing subcomponents or connections during fusion
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
